company_type: Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) - Single-Family Residential Properties

dependencies: Dependence on third parties for key services including maintenance and specialty services (HVAC, roofing, painting, floor installations)
Multi-year contract with third-party vendor for large volume of property services
Reliance on homebuilders for acquisition of newly constructed homes
Dependence on local and national third-party vendors and service providers
risks: Supply chain disruptions and labor shortages could materially impact business operations
Labor inflation and workforce turnover affecting renovation and maintenance
Inability of suppliers and business partners to source labor or supply materials at ordinary levels
Limited number of companies capable of servicing needs at scale
Vendor performance failures could reflect poorly on company reputation
Risk of fraud, misconduct, incompetence, or theft by third-party service providers

regulatory_risks: Fair Housing Act (FHA) compliance and state law counterparts prohibiting discrimination in housing
Municipal regulations, county and city ordinances affecting property use, operation and maintenance
Permit, license, and zoning requirements for property acquisitions and renovations
Broker licensure requirements in each state for real estate leasing transactions
Federal, state, and local accessibility requirements including Americans with Disabilities Act and Fair Housing Act
Affordability covenants on certain properties requiring sale below market value
Eviction, tenant rights, rent control, and rent stabilization laws limiting ability to collect rent, enforce remedies, or increase rental rates
California Tenant Protection Act of 2019 limiting rental rate increases and providing termination protections
Expanding tenant rights laws and restrictions on evictions and collections
Environmental laws and regulations (federal, state, local) regarding hazardous substances, lead-based paint, mold, asbestos
Fire and safety, seismic, asbestos-cleanup, hazardous material abatement requirements
HOA rules and regulations (covenants, conditions and restrictions)

Privacy and data protection laws including California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA), Nevada Privacy Law, Colorado Privacy Act, Utah Consumer Privacy Act, Texas Data Privacy and Security Act
SEC climate-related disclosure requirements (pending effectiveness)
California Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act and Climate-Related Financial Risk Act
REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code Section 856(c)
Risk Retention Rules under Regulation RR requiring retention of 5% credit risk in securitizations
Increased political and regulatory scrutiny of single-family rental industry
Potential governmental inquiries and regulatory proceedings (e.g., FTC investigation)
Inconsistent federal and state regulations creating compliance complexity
